MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Defendant D. Zelinsky And Sons, Inc.'S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Mar-23-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Judiee L. Pinkerton of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing for defendant / moving party D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.
Garen Nazarian of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Willi Schneider and Sandra Schneider

RULING:
DEFENDANT D. ZELINSKY & SONS, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant D. Zelinsky & Sons, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Schneider was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Schneider was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether the sheetrock cement to which defendant's employees exposed Mr. Schneider contained asbestos. Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's Exhibits G and M are sustained. Defendant's objections to the July 31, 2015 deposition testimony of Willi Schneider are sustained. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Willi Schneider are overruled. Defendant's objections to the declaration of Charles Ay are sustained as to paragraphs 134-135 as well as to all the statements throughout Mr. Ay's declaration opining that joint compounds contained asbestos through the mid-to-late 1970s, contained in paragraphs 109, 111, 112, 116, 118, 119, 129-132. Defendant's other objections to Mr. Ay's declaration are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Kimberly R. Hendershott of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12552 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: Defendant Frederick Meiswinkel, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' Negligence II and Loss of Consortium causes of action are denied. Defendant sustained its initial burden pursuant to the factually devoid prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. The deposition testimony and declaration of Willi Schneider and the declaration of Charles Ay create a triable issue whether Mr. Schneider was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (fireproofing) attributable to defendant. Defendant Frederick Meiswinkel, Inc.'s objections to evidence are overruled. Order signed as modified in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Court Reporter: Leticia A.
